title: A Systematic Survey of Prompt Engineering in Large Language Models: Techniques and Applications
author: Pranab Sahoo, Ayush Kumar Singh, Sriparna Saha, Vinija Jain, Samrat Mondal, Aman Chadha
organization: IIT Patna, Stanford University, Amazon AI
extraction_notes: Survey of 41+ distinct prompting techniques categorized by application area. Covers zero-shot through advanced techniques. Key benchmark data: CoT with PaLM 540B achieved 90.2% on math reasoning; self-consistency improves GSM8K by 17.9%; ToT achieves 74% vs CoT 4% on Game of 24; Self-Refine improves GPT-4 by 8.7 points in code optimization.
key_claims: CoT prompting with PaLM 540B achieved 90.2% accuracy on math and commonsense reasoning benchmarks
Auto-CoT surpassed CoT with 1.33% and 1.5% improvements on arithmetic and symbolic reasoning
Self-consistency improves GSM8K by 17.9%, SVAMP by 11.0%, AQuA by 12.2%, StrategyQA by 6.4%
Tree-of-Thoughts achieved 74% success rate vs CoT 4% on Game of 24
Graph-of-Thoughts improved accuracy by 3.41% with T5-base and 5.08% with T5-large on GSM8K
Self-Refine with GPT-4 improved by 8.7 points in code optimization and 21.6 points in sentiment reversal
Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) reduces hallucination through systematic self-verification
Buffer of Thoughts achieves 11% improvement on Game of 24 using only 12% of multi-query computational cost
Code Prompting: GPT-3.5 achieved 8.42 F1 score gain by reformulating NL tasks into structured code
